full_prompt: Design a laser-powder-bed AlSi10Mg satellite payload mounting bracket per a canonical AM aerospace case-study template: minimize mass while surviving quasi-static launch loads at both ultimate and yield safety factors, with an embedded lattice or topology-optimized core. The bracket interfaces a payload attachment pad 100 mm x 100 mm (with four M6 threaded bosses on a 70 x 70 mm square pattern, 6H threads, minimum 12 mm engagement) to a satellite deck rail carrying four M8 bolt holes on a 150 x 80 mm rectangular pattern. The usable design envelope is 200 mm x 150 mm x 80 mm. Material: AM AlSi10Mg per standard LPBF material datasheet (yield 230 MPa, UTS 345 MPa, Young's modulus 68 GPa, density 2670 kg/m^3, elongation 6%), stress-relieved at 300 C. Load cases per NASA-STD-5001B / GEVS random vibration + quasi-static envelope: LC1 quasi-static x-axis 20 g with payload effective mass 5 kg = 980 N applied at payload pad center; LC2 quasi-static y-axis 15 g = 735 N; LC3 quasi-static z-axis 25 g = 1,225 N; LC4 random vibration 14.1 g RMS (GEVS 14.1 g RMS, 20 Hz-2000 Hz) over the four deck-rail bolt holes. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises under any quasi-static case <= UTS / 1.4 = 345 / 1.4 = 246 MPa (NASA-STD-5001B ultimate SF 1.4); (R2) max von Mises <= yield / 1.25 = 230 / 1.25 = 184 MPa (NASA-STD-5001B yield SF 1.25); (R3) final bracket mass <= 150 g (canonical AM showcase target for this envelope, about 65% lighter than bulk AlSi10Mg envelope which would be ~6,400 g); (R4) first natural mode frequency >= 200 Hz (standard space-bracket modal gate to decouple from payload fundamental at ~50 Hz and launcher modes <= 120 Hz); (R5) random vibration response: 3-sigma von Mises under the GEVS 14.1 g RMS PSD <= 246 MPa (same ultimate gate); (R6) AM manufacturability constraints: minimum feature size 0.4 mm, minimum overhang-self-supporting angle 45 deg from build plate, maximum enclosed-void dimension for powder removal 10 mm (standard LPBF AlSi10Mg best-practice limits). Deliverables: STEP AP242 + 3MF of the final part with any lattice baked as solid, STL-high-res for AM build preparation, build orientation callout, and a report with quasi-static per-case max von Mises, mass, first mode frequency, and random-vibration 3-sigma stress.
short_prompt:
Design a laser-powder-bed AlSi10Mg satellite payload mounting bracket per a canonical AM aerospace case-study template: minimize mass while surviving quasi-static launch loads at both ultimate and yield factors, with an embedded lattice or topology-optimized core.

Geometry: payload attachment pad 100 x 100 mm with four M6 threaded bosses on 70 x 70 mm (minimum 12 mm engagement); deck-rail interface with four M8 bolts on 150 x 80 mm. Usable design envelope 200 x 150 x 80 mm.

Material: AM AlSi10Mg (yield 230 MPa, UTS 345 MPa), stress-relieved at 300 C.

Design per NASA-STD-5001B with ultimate SF = 1.4 and yield SF = 1.25, plus a GEVS random-vibration envelope.

Load cases.
- LC1 quasi-static x-axis 20 g at 5 kg payload mass = 980 N.
- LC2 quasi-static y-axis 15 g = 735 N.
- LC3 quasi-static z-axis 25 g = 1,225 N.
- LC4 random vibration 14.1 g RMS, 20 Hz-2000 Hz at the four deck-rail bolt holes.

Additional gates: first natural mode >= 200 Hz; mass <= 150 g; AM manufacturability (min feature 0.4 mm, self-supporting overhang angle 45 deg from build plate, max enclosed-void 10 mm).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Mass target unreachable without topo-opt / lattice; outer loop around the structural FEA.
Overhang + support minimization is an AM-process optimizer, not an FEA physics.

prompt.title: AM AlSi10Mg satellite payload mounting bracket (canonical AM aerospace template)
prompt.brief: Minimize mass of an AM AlSi10Mg satellite payload bracket in a 200x150x80 mm envelope surviving 25 g quasi-static + 14.1 g RMS random per NASA GEVS, with mass target 150 g and first mode >= 200 Hz.

boundary_conditions.payload_pad_center: inertial load applied via 5 kg effective payload mass

pass_fail_criteria.derivation: Canonical AM showcase mass target (approx 65% lighter than bulk envelope)
decoupling from payload fundamental + launcher modes
3-sigma <= ultimate/1.4 under GEVS 14.1 g RMS 20-2000 Hz
feature >= 0.4 mm, overhang >= 45 deg, enclosed void <= 10 mm (LPBF AlSi10Mg)

requirements.governing_standard: NASA-STD-5001B + NASA-STD-7001B (GEVS) + LPBF AlSi10Mg material data sheet + generic AM best practices

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: TET10 for lattice, TET4 for rapid iteration
analysis_types: linear static x3 QS + random vibration PSD

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Thermal-cycle orbital is a separate space-qualification brief.
exclusions_explained.radiation_analysis: Space radiation dose is out of scope here.

notes.numeric_source: NASA-STD-5001B ultimate SF 1.4, yield SF 1.25. GEVS (NASA-STD-7001B) random vibration 14.1 g RMS. LPBF AlSi10Mg datasheet mechanical properties.

eval_coverage: lattice/topology optimization in a tight envelope
